title: Variable reuse to smuggle ecrecover failure signal across assembly boundary
solidity_before:
address signer = ecrecover(hash, v, r, s);
if (signer == address(0)) revert InvalidSignature();
explanation: After the assembly block, the Solidity variable expiry has already been used for its original purpose (timestamp check). Solady reuses it to carry the ecrecover failure flag (iszero(returndatasize())) back into Solidity scope without declaring a new variable. The final check combines nonce mismatch and ecrecover failure into one OR expression, saving a separate if-revert branch.
when_to_apply: EIP-712 signature verification where a Solidity variable is no longer needed after the assembly block
when_not_to_apply: When variable reuse obscures intent in security-critical code that others must audit
